title: Mod Loading & Script Injection

steps: Scan modDirectory for .jar/.zip files and directories containing mod.json/mod.hjson/plugin.hjson
Add Steam Workshop mods via platform.getWorkshopContent()
For each candidate: findMeta(resolveRoot(file)) → parse ModMeta (name, version, main class, dependencies)
[GUARD] meta == null or meta.name == null → skip candidate
resolveDependencies(metas) → topological sort; outputs Map<name, ModState>
For each resolved entry: loadMod(file, overwrite=false, initialize=(state==enabled))
[ERROR] load fails → log error, skip mod (continue loop)
sortMods() → sort by state ordinal then name
buildFiles() → walk each enabled mod root, addFile() all non-special-folder files into FileTree
Load bundle .properties files into I18NBundle chain
resolveRoot: if directory use as-is, else wrap in ZipFi
[CONDITION] mod with same baseName already in mods list:
[overwrite && !hasSteamID] → close classloader, delete old zip/dir, remove from list
[CONDITION] Desktop → traverse class path segments to find .class file
Return LoadedMod(root=zip, file=sourceFile, meta, main=mainMod, loader)
packSprites() → submit async tasks: read Pixmap, optionally bleed(2px), queue for packing
If mod.main != null → mod.main.packSprites(packer) (Java mod custom sprites)
Await all futures; pack each pixmap into MultiPacker pages
waitForMain: dispose old Core.atlas, install shadow atlas for icon generation
For each mod content (UnlockableContent): u.load(), u.loadIcon(), u.createIcons(packer)
waitForMain: flush packer → new TextureAtlas, apply texture scale overrides
loadSync() → loadIcons(): load icon.png texture for each mod
For each mod in orderedMods() where mod.main!=null && !mod.meta.hidden:
mod.main.loadContent() → mod registers Content objects (Items, Blocks, etc.)
For each enabled mod: scan mod.root/content/{type}s/ for .json/.hjson files
Respect meta.contentOrder: ordered files loaded first, rest sorted alphabetically
→ ContentLoader.handleContent(content): assign ID, add to contentMap
→ ContentLoader.handleMappableContent(content): prefix name with mod name via transformName(), add to nameMap
[ERROR] duplicate name → remove from list, throw IllegalArgumentException
[CONDITION] scripts==null → scripts = platform.createScripts() (lazy init Rhino engine)
run(global.js, false) → loads all importPackage() + EventType consts
[ERROR] Throwable → log error at LogLevel.err, return false
Android O+ (SDK >= 26): use InMemoryAndroidClassLoader (holds DEX in ByteBuffer)
Android < O: use FileAndroidClassLoader (writes .dex to cache file)
[CONDITION] global already set but not AndroidContextFactory → throw IllegalStateException
factory.enterContext() → returns Rhino Context ready for use
DexOptions → DexFile → DirectClassFile → CfTranslator.translate() → Java bytecode to DEX
If existing DEX: DexMerger.merge([newDex, oldDex], KEEP_FIRST)
onContextReleased(cx): BaseAndroidClassLoader.reset() (delete file or set last=null)
createBaseContent(): loads all vanilla content (Items, Blocks, Units, Bullets, Planets, etc.)
init(): initialize(Content::init) then initialize(Content::postInit), logicVars.init(), Events.fire(ContentInitEvent)
initialize(callable): idempotent (tracks called set); iterates all ContentType.all × contentMap; error in mod content → handleContentError, error in base → rethrow
addFile(path, fi): normalize path (backslash→slash), store in ObjectMap<String,Fi>
get(path, safe): check files map (with and without leading /), fallback to Core.files.internal(path)
clear(): wipe all mod file entries (called on reload)
resolve(fileName): delegates to get() — implements FileHandleResolver for asset system
Scan classpath packages: mindustry.*, arc.func, arc.struct, arc.scene, arc.math, arc.audio, arc.input, arc.util, arc.files, arc.flabel
Add explicit whitelist classes (Draw, Fill, Color, Core, Mathf, Call, etc.)
Filter: remove synthetic, anonymous, private, blacklisted packages (tools, arc.flabel.effects)
Generate importPackage(Packages.xxx) for each unique package
For each EventType inner class: generate 'const ClassName = Packages.mindustry.game.EventType.ClassName'
Also generate ClassMap.java: ObjectMap<String,Class<?>> of simple name → concrete class for JSON mod use
[CONDITION] any newImport has missing/soft dependencies → checkDependencies() → show auto-import dialog
On app restart: full pipeline re-runs from phase 1 (discovery → build files → pack sprites → load content → load scripts)

key_relationships: Mods owns ContentParser (for JSON content parsing)
Mods uses FileTree (tree.addFile()) to register mod asset paths
Mods calls ContentLoader.setCurrentMod() before each mod's content registration
Scripts uses Rhino Context + Scriptable scope for JS evaluation
AndroidRhinoContext provides Android-specific Rhino Context via ContextFactory override
ScriptMainGenerator (build-time) generates global.js consumed by Scripts constructor at runtime
ScriptMainGenerator generates ClassMap.java consumed by ContentParser for JSON type resolution
FileTree implements FileHandleResolver → plugged into Core.assets
LoadedMod.main (Mod subclass) provides loadContent() hook and packSprites() hook
ModClassLoader extends ClassLoader, child of mainLoader
key_conditions_and_branches: Android vs Desktop: different ClassLoader strategy (DEX vs JVM bytecode)
Android O+ vs older: InMemoryAndroidClassLoader vs FileAndroidClassLoader
allScripts.size==1: use that single file as main; else require scripts/main.js
scripts==null: lazy-init Rhino engine on first scripted mod encounter
skipModCode flag: bypasses all Java class loading and JS execution
meta.hidden: Java mod main loaded but loadContent() skipped
contentOrder in meta: deterministic JSON content load ordering per mod
overwrite flag in loadMod: controls whether existing mod is replaced or error thrown
global.js parse error → Scripts.errored=true → warning shown on ClientLoadEvent
important_notes: Rhino JS engine is used for scripting (not Nashorn/GraalVM). global.js is pre-generated at build time by ScriptMainGenerator and bundled in core/assets/scripts/. Each mod script runs wrapped in an IIFE with 'use strict'. Android requires DEX compilation of Rhino-generated bytecode (Java bytecode is not directly executable). Mod file tree is flat-mapped by path into FileTree which resolves mod overrides before falling back to internal assets. Content names are prefixed with mod name (e.g. 'mymod-myblock') via ContentLoader.transformName().
